# Per-page template scoping catches real type errors within a specific page.
# The owners page accesses .Missing which doesn't exist on OwnersPage,
# while the vehicles page is fine.

! check-templates ./...
stderr 'list\.html.*Missing not found on example\.com/app\.OwnersPage \(E001\)'

-- go.mod --
module example.com/app

go 1.25.0
-- main.go --
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"html/template"
	"net/http"
	"path/filepath"
)

type OwnersPage struct {
	Title  string
	Owners []string
}

type VehiclesPage struct {
	Title    string
	Vehicles []string
}

func loadTemplates(root string) map[string]*template.Template {
	layoutFile := filepath.Join(root, "layouts", "base.html")
	pageFiles, _ := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(root, "pages", "*", "*.html"))
	templates := make(map[string]*template.Template)
	for _, page := range pageFiles {
		name := filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(page)) + "/" + filepath.Base(page)
		files := []string{layoutFile, page}
		t, err := template.ParseFiles(files...)
		if err != nil {
			panic(err)
		}
		templates[name] = t
	}
	return templates
}

func main() {
	templates := loadTemplates("templates")

	render := func(w http.ResponseWriter, name string, data any) {
		t, ok := templates[name]
		if !ok {
			http.Error(w, "not found", 500)
			return
		}
		_ = t.ExecuteTemplate(w, "base.html", data)
	}

	http.HandleFunc("/owners", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		render(w, "owners/list.html", OwnersPage{Title: "Owners", Owners: []string{"Alice"}})
	})
	http.HandleFunc("/vehicles", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		render(w, "vehicles/list.html", VehiclesPage{Title: "Vehicles", Vehicles: []string{"Car"}})
	})
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint

-- templates/layouts/base.html --
<h1>{{.Title}}</h1>
{{template "content" .}}

-- templates/pages/owners/list.html --
{{define "content"}}
<ul>{{range .Owners}}<li>{{.}}</li>{{end}}</ul>
<p>{{.Missing}}</p>
{{end}}

-- templates/pages/vehicles/list.html --
{{define "content"}}
<ul>{{range .Vehicles}}<li>{{.}}</li>{{end}}</ul>
{{end}}
